final:
## Fund Manager Decision

### 1. INDEPENDENT OBSERVATION

The round-trip cost of this trade needs acknowledgment. Entry fees + exit fees on Bybit (taker ~0.055% each side) total ~0.11%, meaning the +0.17% gross P&L nets to roughly **+0.06% after fees** — effectively a scratch trade. The real cost isn't monetary; it's the process failure that allowed a 0.47x vol_ma_ratio entry to slip past in the first place. I note that this is the **third consecutive sub-1% outcome** following the -0.98% and -0.14% losses, all sharing the same root cause: entering without volume confirmation. The pattern is clear and the Trader is right to self-correct.

Additionally: FOMC is March 18. Holding any directional position through the next 4 days without a defined stop — which the current long lacks — is unacceptable portfolio management regardless of thesis quality.

### 2. ORDERBOOK / LIQUIDITY CHECK

Closing a small long at ~$69,973 on BTC/USDT perpetual on Bybit presents zero liquidity concern. Daily volume exceeds $5B. Our position size relative to book depth is negligible. Market exit will fill cleanly with minimal slippage (<0.01%).

### 3. EXPLICIT AGREEMENT / DISAGREEMENT

**I AGREE with the Trader's thesis** because the position was opened in violation of our own hard entry rule (vol_ma_ratio 0.47x vs 0.8x minimum), and continuing to hold a rule-violating position through peak event risk (FOMC + yield surge + DXY at 100) compounds the original error. The Trader correctly identified that correcting a process failure at +0.17% is vastly preferable to learning the lesson again at -1% to -3%. Cash is itself a position — and in this environment, it's the right one.

---

**Overall Assessment:** This is a clean, disciplined exit. The Trader caught their own rule violation, the Risk Judge confirmed elevated event risk with a poor Sharpe (0.04), and the risk sizing block validates zero exposure post-exit. The re-entry conditions are well-defined and appropriately gated behind FOMC res
